name: Trie — Prefix Tree for Autocomplete and Word Search

metadata.career_relevance: Trie is the go-to data structure for string prefix problems. Word Search II (Trie+backtracking) is a Google hard. Autocomplete is implemented in every production search box — Flipkart, Zerodha, Amazon all use Trie variants.

hook.what_you_will_build: Trie with insert/search/startsWith/autocomplete, Word Search II, Replace Words.

concept_explanation.trie: Trie node: ch[26] + boolean end. Insert: traverse creating nodes. Search: traverse checking end flag. StartsWith: traverse without checking end.

key_takeaways: Trie node: Trie[] ch=new Trie[26]; boolean end
Insert: O(m) — create nodes for each char
startsWith: O(m) — traverse, return true if reached (no end check)
Autocomplete: DFS from prefix endpoint, collect end-marked words
Word Search II: build Trie of target words, DFS on grid with Trie traversal
Replace Words: stop at first complete word in Trie
MapSum: store cumulative val at each Trie node; insert updates diff
reflection.next_topic_preview: Module 35 complete! Next: M36 DSA8 — Heaps & Priority Queues.
